title: Folio
description: The minimal digital whiteboard for sketching and prototyping.

blocks.heading: Your Sketching Suite.
blocks.description: An infinite canvas built for clarity. Sketch ideas, wireframe interfaces, and prototype flows — all in one place.

callToAction.hint: No signup. No install. Ready in seconds.

pillars.heading: Private by design.
pillars.description: Your work stays yours. Folio stores everything in your browser's local storage — no accounts, no servers, no tracking. What you draw never leaves your device.

pillars.heading: An infinite canvas.
pillars.description: No boundaries, no grids, no constraints. Pan, zoom, and sketch freely on a canvas that grows with your ideas — however big or complex they get.

pillars.heading: Keep ideas organized.
pillars.description: Group related work into pages within a single board. Switch between them instantly, present them in sequence, or use them to break down a project into stages.

pillars.heading: Build your own library.
pillars.description: Save shapes, components, and layouts you use often. Import and export collections to reuse across boards or share with your team — your personal design system, always at hand.

pillars.heading: Folio Lite
pillars.description: Open your browser and start drawing. No account, no setup, no friction. Perfect for quick ideas, sketches on the fly, or anyone who just wants to get started immediately.

pillars.heading: Folio Studio
pillars.description: Run Folio on your own machine or server via Docker. Unlimited boards, full data ownership, and zero external dependencies. Built for teams and power users who want complete control.

items.question: Is Folio free?
items.answer: Yes, completely. Folio Lite runs in your browser with no cost, no account, and no hidden limits. Folio Studio is also free and open source — you just need to host it yourself.

items.question: Do I need to create an account?
items.answer: No. Folio Lite works entirely in your browser without any signup. Just open the app and start drawing. Folio Studio is self-hosted, so there's no central account system either.

items.question: Where is my data stored?
items.answer: In Folio Lite, everything is saved in your browser's local storage — nothing is sent to any server. In Folio Studio, data is stored on your own machine or server, depending on where you deploy it.

items.question: What's the difference between Folio Lite and Folio Studio?
items.answer: Folio Lite runs directly in your browser — no setup needed, but limited to a single board. Folio Studio is a self-hosted version you deploy with Docker, which adds unlimited boards, a home screen with thumbnails, and full control over your data and infrastructure.

items.question: Can I use Folio offline?
items.answer: Folio Lite works offline once the page has loaded, since everything runs client-side. Folio Studio, being self-hosted, works completely offline on your local network.

items.question: Is Folio open source?
items.answer: Yes. Folio is fully open source and available on GitHub. You can inspect the code, report issues, suggest features, or contribute directly to the project.

blocks.label: Folio Studio
blocks.heading: Sketching. Self-Hosted.
blocks.description: A self-hosted whiteboard for creators and teams who want full control over their tools and data — no cloud, no compromises.

pillars.heading: Full Folio, no compromises.
pillars.description: Every feature from Folio Lite, running entirely on your own infrastructure. No external dependencies, no third-party services, no surprises.

pillars.heading: Your data stays with you.
pillars.description: No cloud sync, no telemetry, no tracking. Folio Studio stores everything locally on your server — you decide who has access and what happens to your files.

pillars.heading: Unlimited boards.
pillars.description: Create as many boards as you need. Organize projects, explorations, and prototypes in separate spaces — no artificial limits.

pillars.heading: A sidebar that does it all.
pillars.description: Rename, duplicate, download, or delete boards in one click. Your full workspace is always one panel away.

pillars.heading: Visual board previews.
pillars.description: Your home screen shows live thumbnails of recent boards so you always know exactly where to pick up.

pillars.heading: Import and export freely.
pillars.description: Move boards between Folio Lite and Studio without friction. Export your work at any time — your files are always portable.

items.question: How do I install Folio Studio?
items.answer: Folio Studio is distributed as a Docker image. Pull it with docker pull ghcr.io/jmjuanes/folio-studio and follow the setup instructions in the documentation.

items.question: What are the server requirements?
items.answer: Folio Studio is lightweight and runs on any machine that supports Docker — including a Raspberry Pi, a personal laptop, or a VPS. No special hardware is required.

items.question: Can I migrate my boards from Folio Lite?
items.answer: Yes. You can export your board from Folio Lite and import it directly into Folio Studio. The format is fully compatible between both versions.

items.question: Is Folio Studio also free?
items.answer: Yes. Folio Studio is free and open source, just like Folio Lite. You only pay for the infrastructure you choose to run it on.

items.question: Can multiple people use the same Folio Studio instance?
items.answer: Folio Studio is currently designed as a personal or small-team tool. Multi-user collaboration with real-time sync is not yet supported, but it's on the roadmap.

blocks.heading: What's New.
blocks.description: Every improvement, fix, and new feature — in one place.
